variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: You owe $10,000,000 to a ruthless criminal lender at 45% interest compounding daily, with the full balance due immediately and your family's safety pledged as collateral.

variations.label: low
variations.text: You owe $10,000,000 at 25% annual interest with a strict 1-year repayment deadline, and all your assets will be seized if you miss a single payment.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: You owe $10,000,000 at 15% annual interest on a rigid 3-year repayment schedule with substantial penalties for any late or missed payments.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: You owe $10,000,000 at 3% annual interest with a flexible 10-year repayment plan and no prepayment penalties.

variations.label: high
variations.text: You owe $10,000,000 at 0% interest with a 30-year repayment schedule and the option to defer payments during periods of financial hardship.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: You owe $10,000,000 at 0% interest with no fixed repayment deadline, and the lender has indicated they are willing to forgive the balance entirely.

quality_checks.reasoning: All tiers describe owing $10,000,000 but vary the severity of the debt terms—interest rate, repayment timeline, penalties, and lender disposition. Each step meaningfully improves the borrower's situation, from life-threatening loan-shark terms (tier 1) to a virtually forgivable debt (tier 7). The core scenario (a $10M debt) is preserved throughout.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because a $10M debt with 0% interest, no deadline, and likely forgiveness is dramatically better than the same debt under punitive, dangerous, or rigid terms. A rational agent always prefers milder debt terms.
